description: Growing a vector with c() or append inside a loop copies the whole structure each step, turning work quadratic.
body: Failure mode: a loop accumulates results by reassigning x <- c(x, new) each iteration, copying the entire vector every step so runtime grows quadratically with n. Detection signals: runtime that explodes as input size grows; high memory churn; a loop that is fine on small data but hangs on large. Do not grow vectors incrementally in a loop. Safer fallback: preallocate the result to its final length and assign by index, or vectorize the computation entirely.
